improvements.expected_impact: Removes friction in the core 'paste this config into your MCP client' task and avoids a localhost value looking like the production endpoint.

improvements.recommendation: Audit the shared AppHeader/footer for the heading-order source (e.g. a footer h-level that skips) since this page's own outline is clean; fixing it at the chrome level clears heading-order across the whole cluster.
improvements.expected_impact: Likely resolves the heading-order(1) flag on all four help/static pages simultaneously rather than page-by-page.

improvements.recommendation: Swap the literal hex values for the existing design tokens (--border-subtle, neutral text/secondary tokens) and, where practical, build the section cards on the shared .public-panel primitive so shell token updates propagate.
improvements.expected_impact: Stops palette drift, shrinks the bespoke CSS, and keeps the strongest page locked to the token system.

improvements.recommendation: Keep the page as the reference template for the other help surfaces: its h1>h2 discipline, monospace code/URL, <dl> and <pre> semantics, and decoration-free density are the model About/Documentation should converge toward.
improvements.expected_impact: Codifies a quiet technical-reference pattern other help pages can reuse, raising cluster consistency.

one_line_verdict: A bare third-party Swagger UI bolted onto the app with none of the SysNDD shell, tokens, or chrome — functional as an API explorer but the clear consistency and accessibility outlier of the cluster.

top_findings.issue: No SysNDD shell or design-token alignment at all: ApiView is a raw SwaggerUIBundle mount (#swagger-ui) with no public-hero, no public-kicker, no route-level h1 wrapper, and Swagger's own typography/spacing/colors. It sits flush against the app header with no page padding container, so it visually reads as a different product than every sibling help page.
top_findings.evidence: ApiView.vue:1-9 template is just container-fluid + #swagger-ui; the only scoped style is a .version color override (:50-52); no public-page class, unlike About/Documentation/MCP.

top_findings.issue: color-contrast(5): Swagger's default palette fails contrast on at least five elements — the gray version pill '0.21.6', muted /api/admin/openapi.json link, the section toggle chevrons, and low-contrast gray on white. The green 'OAS 3.0' badge and green 'Authorize' button also use Swagger green, not the SysNDD success token, and the green-on-light-green badge is borderline.
top_findings.evidence: Lighthouse color-contrast(5); screenshot shows gray '0.21.6' pill, gray openapi.json link, faint chevrons, Swagger-green OAS 3.0 / Authorize against light backgrounds (no SysNDD token override beyond the .version hack).

top_findings.issue: Heading hierarchy comes entirely from Swagger: the visible 'SysNDD API' is Swagger's <h2>, there is no app-owned route h1, and the tag sections (default/health/version/entity/review...) are Swagger's own heading structure, producing the heading-order(1) failure and breaking the 'exactly one route-level h1' invariant.
top_findings.evidence: ApiView.vue renders only #swagger-ui; no h1 in template; Lighthouse heading-order(1); design intent requires exactly ONE route-level h1.

top_findings.issue: Mismatched version metadata erodes trust: the Swagger title shows API version '0.21.6' while the app header shows v0.21.7 in the same viewport, a visible inconsistency on a page whose whole purpose is authoritative API reference.
top_findings.evidence: Screenshot: '0.21.6' pill next to 'SysNDD API' vs 'v0.21.7' in the masthead; AppVersionInfo elsewhere surfaces app/api/db versions but Swagger pulls its own from openapi.json.

top_findings.issue: Loading/error states are minimal and unstyled: a plain 'Loading API documentation...' line and a Bootstrap alert-warning, but initSwagger sets loading=false synchronously after the call (ApiView.vue:38) so the spinner logic doesn't actually track Swagger's async fetch, and a slow/failed openapi.json fetch won't reliably surface the error branch.
top_findings.evidence: ApiView.vue:27-44: loading set false in the try block immediately after SwaggerUIBundle() call returns, not on Swagger render/fetch completion; error only catches a synchronous throw.

improvements.recommendation: Wrap Swagger in the shared public-page shell: add a public-hero with the SysNDD kicker, a single route-level <h1> ('SysNDD API'), and a one-line description, then mount #swagger-ui inside a public-panel so the page inherits the app's container, padding, and visual identity.
improvements.expected_impact: Restores the one-h1 invariant, makes the page recognizably SysNDD, and likely resolves heading-order by giving the document a proper app-owned h1>h2 outline above Swagger's content.

improvements.recommendation: Add a scoped Swagger theme overriding its palette to SysNDD tokens — primary medical-blue for links/headers, success #2e7d32 for Authorize/OAS badge, and darker neutral text for version pills/chevrons — targeting the five contrast-failing elements specifically.
improvements.expected_impact: Clears color-contrast(5), lifts the a11y score from 95, and stops the page from looking like default Swagger green/gray inside a blue-tokened app.

improvements.recommendation: Source the Swagger info.version from the same release version the app header uses (or hide Swagger's version pill and show the unified AppVersionInfo) so API/app/db versions agree on screen.
improvements.expected_impact: Removes the 0.21.6-vs-0.21.7 mismatch and reinforces trust on the canonical API reference; benefits anywhere version is shown.

improvements.recommendation: Track Swagger's real onComplete/onFailure to drive loading and error, and style the loading and error states with the app's spinner and problem+json error treatment instead of bare text/alert.
improvements.expected_impact: Accurate loading/error feedback when openapi.json is slow or unavailable, matching the interaction-state quality of the other help pages.

one_line_verdict: A genuinely well-composed two-panel sign-in: quiet, trustworthy, brand-anchored context paired with a focused form — let down only by a heading-order slip and a duplicated 18px title token across both panels.

top_findings.issue: Heading-order violation: the page-title <h1> 'Curator sign in' lives in the left context panel, while the actual form heading 'Sign in' is an <h2 id=login-title> — but visually 'Sign in' reads as the dominant/primary action and both use the identical .login-title style, so the h1->h2 jump is arbitrary rather than semantic.
top_findings.evidence: Lighthouse heading-order(1); LoginView.vue lines 21 (h1 'Curator sign in') and 45 (h2 'Sign in'), both class='login-title' so they render at the same visual weight.

top_findings.issue: Two equally-weighted titles compete for the eye. 'Curator sign in' (left) and 'Sign in' (right) are the same size/weight, so neither clearly owns the page; the left kicker 'SYSNDD ACCOUNT' and right 'SECURE ACCESS' add a third and fourth label layer.
top_findings.evidence: Screenshot shows two near-identical bold ~28-30px titles side by side; both map to .login-title in LoginView.vue.

top_findings.issue: Field help text ('Enter your user name', 'Enter your user password') is redundant with the visible labels and adds vertical noise without informing the expert user.
top_findings.evidence: BFormGroup description props at LoginView.vue lines 54 and 72 render greyed helper lines visible under each input in the screenshot.

top_findings.issue: Validation state is wired (vee-validate touched/error), but there is no visible inline error styling cue in the resting screenshot, and the only error feedback for failed auth is a transient toast plus a shake animation — easy to miss for keyboard/AT users.
top_findings.evidence: LoginView.vue lines 61/80 bind :state; failure path is makeToast + .shake (lines 256, 300-322) with no persistent inline summary.

improvements.recommendation:
Promote the form's 'Sign in' to the single route-level

(or merge the two panels under one h1) and demote the left-panel 'Curator sign in' to an

/styled

, so heading order matches visual weight and Lighthouse heading-order clears.
improvements.expected_impact: Fixes the a11y heading-order audit and resolves the dual-title hierarchy ambiguity.

improvements.recommendation: Drop the redundant BFormGroup description text on Username/Password (or replace with format hints only when non-obvious), keeping labels as the single source of field meaning.
improvements.expected_impact: Reduces vertical noise; tightens the form to a clinical-tool density without losing clarity.

improvements.recommendation: Add a persistent inline auth-error region (aria-live polite) above or below the form instead of relying on a fleeting toast + shake, so failed logins are announced and remain visible.
improvements.expected_impact: Improves accessibility and error recovery across all auth views that share the same toast pattern.

improvements.recommendation: Define a shared type-scale token for page vs section titles (e.g. .page-title 20px / .section-title 16px) and apply distinct tokens to the two panels so they are no longer byte-identical.
improvements.expected_impact: Establishes reusable title hierarchy tokens that also help register and other shell pages.

one_line_verdict: A clean Lighthouse score masks a dated, inconsistent form: a heavy near-black header bar, no <h1>, label-less inputs that rely on placeholders + below-field captions, and dark buttons that diverge sharply from the Login page's blue primary system.

top_findings.issue: Inputs have NO real <label> — every field relies on a placeholder for the name and a below-field BFormGroup description for the hint. Placeholders disappear on focus/typing, so the field identity is lost while filling, and the caption-below-input pattern is the inverse of conventional label-above.
top_findings.evidence: RegisterView.vue BFormGroup blocks (lines 16-80) set only `description` + BFormInput `placeholder`; no `label`/`label-for`. Screenshot shows 'Username', 'mail@your-institution.com', 'NNNN-...' only as grey placeholders with the explanatory text beneath.

top_findings.issue: No route-level <h1>. The page's title is delivered as a BCard `header` ('Register new SysNDD account') rendered inside a dark bar — semantically not a heading, so the page has no h1 at all, unlike Login which at least has one.
top_findings.evidence: RegisterView.vue line 9 header attribute on BCard; no <h1> anywhere in template. (Lighthouse a11y100 is misleading here — bvn card-header is a div, so the missing-h1/heading-order rule simply finds nothing to order.)

top_findings.issue: Color/shape system diverges from the rest of Auth and the design tokens: a near-black (header-bg-variant='dark') header bar and `variant='dark'` Register button vs Login's medical-blue primary. This is decorative heavy chrome the brief explicitly warns against and breaks cross-page consistency.
top_findings.evidence: RegisterView.vue lines 10-11 (dark header bg/white text), line 104 variant='dark'; Login uses variant='primary'. Screenshot shows a black title bar and black Register button — no #0d47a1/#1565c0 blue.

top_findings.issue: The terms error 'You must accept the terms' is shown in red on initial render before any interaction, reading like a failure state on a pristine form.
top_findings.evidence: Screenshot shows red 'You must accept the terms' under an unchecked box at first paint; terms_agreed is initialized to 'not_accepted' (line 216) and the validator returns the message eagerly.

top_findings.issue: Centered captions and an unchecked checkbox floating left of a centered 'I accept the terms' label create a misaligned, off-grid layout; the form lacks the left-aligned label rhythm of a data-entry tool.
top_findings.evidence: Screenshot: all description lines are centered under inputs, the checkbox sits far left while its label is centered, producing an unbalanced column.

improvements.recommendation: Add explicit `label` + `label-for` to every BFormGroup (Username, Email, ORCID, First/Family name, Comment) and keep the existing description as a true hint below the label; never rely on placeholder-as-label.
improvements.expected_impact: Fixes the most serious a11y/usability gap, keeps field identity visible while typing, and matches the Login form's labelled pattern.

improvements.recommendation: Replace the BCard `header`/dark-bar title with a real route-level <h1> (e.g. 'Register a SysNDD account') above a borderless or --border-subtle card, removing header-bg-variant='dark'.
improvements.expected_impact: Gives the page a genuine h1, removes heavy decorative chrome, and aligns visual weight with the rest of the app.

improvements.recommendation: Switch Reset/Register buttons to the shared outline-secondary / primary (medical-blue) variants used on Login so auth actions are visually identical across pages.
improvements.expected_impact: Unifies primary-action color across all auth/form pages; eliminates the one-off dark variant.

improvements.recommendation: Suppress validation messages until a field is touched or the form is submitted (use the same touched-gated :state pattern Login already uses) so the terms error does not show on first paint.
improvements.expected_impact: Removes the false-error first impression and aligns error-timing behavior across forms.

improvements.recommendation: Left-align all field labels, descriptions, and the checkbox row to a single column grid (12px form-group gaps) to read as a data-entry tool rather than a centered marketing form.
improvements.expected_impact: Restores the compact, scannable rhythm expected of the SysNDD design language.

one_line_verdict: An excellent, dense, table-first clinical gene page — header chips, resource rail, ACMG ClinVar chips and a genomic lollipop all read as a serious research tool — but it is dragged down by a severe aria-prohibited-attr storm (636 nodes) rooted in the shared badge/identifier components.

top_findings.issue: Massive aria-prohibited-attr violation (636 nodes): the shared badge components put `role="link"`/`role="img"` on a <span> while ALSO wiring `v-b-tooltip` (which injects aria-describedby/title) and an `aria-label`. role=link/img with these combinations, repeated across every chip in the resource rail, identifier strip, and the ~57-entity associated table, multiplies into hundreds of identical prohibited-attribute nodes.
top_findings.evidence: Lighthouse aria-prohibited-attr(636!); GeneBadge.vue lines 6-9 (role='link' + :title + aria-label on a span inside a BLink anchor), DiseaseBadge.vue 5-12, InheritanceBadge.vue 4-11 (role='img'). The count scales with the entity table density visible in the screenshot.

top_findings.issue: CLS 0.198 on a content-rich page: the header card, Associated table, and three external SectionCards hydrate asynchronously with min-heights set per-card, but the entities table mounts before its row count is known and external cards swap skeleton->content, causing visible layout shift.
top_findings.evidence: Lighthouse CLS 0.198; GeneView.vue deferred externalsReady (lines 241-251) and SectionCard min-height props (16rem) vs the variable-height TablesEntities mounted at line 70 without a reserved height.

top_findings.issue: Nested-card stacking: the header BCard wraps ClinicalResourcesCard + IdentifierCard, and below it three SectionCard-wrapped external cards sit in their own bordered frames, plus the genomic-viz card — a lot of competing card borders in one viewport that slightly muddies the single-surface intent.
top_findings.evidence: GeneView.vue header BCard (lines 20-63) contains two child cards; the frameless SectionCard pattern (lines 84-140) helps but the screenshot still shows ~5 bordered regions stacked.

top_findings.issue: One residual color-contrast failure plus several status chips lean on color: the ClinVar 'LP' chip uses orange #f97316 with dark #111827 text and 'VUS' uses #ffc107 — the short labels (P/LP/VUS/LB/B) carry meaning but the borderline-contrast orange/amber chips are the likely color-contrast(1) hit.
top_findings.evidence: Lighthouse color-contrast(1); GeneClinVarCard.vue .clinvar-chip--lp background #f97316 / color #111827 and --vus #ffc107 / #212529 (lines 296-304).

top_findings.issue: Mobile reflow is genuinely good (single column, full identifier list, chips wrap), but the brain-DNA logo + hamburger header consumes a tall band and the 'Associated' card shows a single entity rendered as a stacked chip cluster that is workable but visually loose.
top_findings.evidence: gene-detail-mobile.png shows clean single-column stacking; entity row becomes a vertical chip group (sysndd:57 / ARID1B / Coffin-Siris / AD / Definitive / NDD Yes).

improvements.recommendation: Refactor the shared badge components (GeneBadge, DiseaseBadge, InheritanceBadge, EntityBadge) to remove role='link'/role='img' from spans: when linked, let the wrapping anchor carry the name; for non-interactive labels drop the role and use plain text + aria-hidden icon. Move tooltips off the role-bearing element.
improvements.expected_impact: Eliminates the 636-node aria-prohibited-attr storm here and proportional violations on every entity/gene/table page that reuses these badges — single highest-leverage a11y fix in the app.

improvements.recommendation: Reserve vertical space for the Associated table (skeleton with a fixed row-height * expected rows, or a min-height equal to the loaded state) so the largest async block stops shifting layout.
improvements.expected_impact: Cuts CLS below 0.1 here and on entity-detail/other SWR detail pages that mount tables before counts are known.

improvements.recommendation: Retune the ClinVar chip palette to AA-compliant tokens: deepen LP/VUS (e.g. amber-on-dark or darker amber) and verify each chip's text/bg pair against 4.5:1, keeping the P/LP/VUS short labels as the icon-free color-independent signal.
improvements.expected_impact: Clears the color-contrast(1) audit and hardens the pathogenicity legend used wherever ClinVar chips appear.

improvements.recommendation: Reduce competing borders by making the header's child cards frameless (they already render inside the header BCard) and confirming the three external cards are the only --border-subtle frames in that band.
improvements.expected_impact: Calms the stacked-border look and reinforces the single-surface clinical aesthetic.

improvements.recommendation: Audit and fix the remaining heading-order(1): ensure the gene-page <h1> (symbol) is the only h1 and that card titles use a consistent non-heading or stepped-heading token rather than skipping levels.
improvements.expected_impact: Resolves the heading-order audit on every SectionCard-based detail page at once.

one_line_verdict: The captured screenshot is a bare '404 Page Not Found' for /Entities/1, so the rich entity layout in source never rendered — scoring reflects the failed route plus the source-level header-order and chip-a11y patterns the page would inherit.

top_findings.issue: The audited route returned 404. /Entities/1 redirected to /PageNotFound (the source watcher routes to /PageNotFound when the entity record resolves to null), meaning entity_id=1 has no approved public record, so the audit captured an empty error page instead of the intended entity-detail UI.
top_findings.evidence: entity-detail.png shows only large '404 / Page Not Found' centered text; EntityView.vue lines 454-463 push to /PageNotFound on resolved-null. The 5 failed network requests noted in Lighthouse corroborate the empty record.

top_findings.issue: The 404 page itself is extremely sparse: two centered text lines, no recovery affordance (no search box focus, no 'back to Entities', no suggested links) — poor empty/error UX for what an expert will hit on any stale entity link.
top_findings.evidence: entity-detail.png: '404' + 'Page Not Found' with nothing else between the global header and footer; no CTA.

top_findings.issue: Heading-order(1) is reported for this route; the 404 view's '404' and 'Page Not Found' likely render as same-level or non-sequential headings, and (when the real page renders) EntityView wraps its <h1> 'Entity' inside a SectionCard header with multiple sibling card titles, risking order issues.
top_findings.evidence: Lighthouse heading-order(1); EntityView.vue line 27 h1 inside SectionCard #header slot, with five sibling SectionCard titles (Clinical Synopsis, Publications, Gene Reviews, Phenotypes, Variation) that are non-heading divs.

top_findings.issue: Source-level inherited risk: the entity hero reuses the same role-bearing badges (GeneBadge/DiseaseBadge/InheritanceBadge) plus many anchor chips with `data-tooltip` CSS pseudo-tooltips and aria-labels — the same aria-prohibited-attr family that scored 636 on gene-detail would recur once the page renders with data.
top_findings.evidence: EntityView.vue lines 49-62 (badges) and the chip anchors at 148-258 (aria-label + data-tooltip); GeneBadge/Disease/Inheritance role attributes as in gene-detail findings.

top_findings.issue: Cannot assess the actual entity dataviz/spacing because it did not render; the design in source (3-column gene/inheritance/disease unit grid, classification pill, chip panels) is promising but unverified in this audit pass.
top_findings.evidence: No entity content in entity-detail.png; layout only inferable from EntityView.vue template (lines 45-260).

improvements.recommendation: Re-run the audit against a known-good public entity id (e.g. one returned by the entity table) so the real entity-detail layout is captured and can be scored; entity_id=1 is not a valid public record.
improvements.expected_impact: Produces a meaningful audit of the actual page instead of the 404 fallback.

improvements.recommendation: Make the 404/PageNotFound view useful: add a route-level <h1>, a short message, a focused global search, and quick links (Entities table, Genes, Home) so stale entity/gene deep-links recover gracefully.
improvements.expected_impact: Improves error recovery for every bad deep link across the app, not just entities.

improvements.recommendation: When the real page renders, hoist the entity <h1> out of the SectionCard header into a proper page-title slot and make the five evidence-card titles consistent non-heading or correctly-stepped headings to clear heading-order.
improvements.expected_impact: Fixes heading-order on entity-detail and the shared SectionCard title pattern used across detail pages.

improvements.recommendation: Apply the shared badge a11y refactor (remove role from spans, move tooltips off the named element) so the entity hero and chip panels do not reproduce the aria-prohibited-attr storm once populated.
improvements.expected_impact: Pre-empts the same 600+ node a11y violation on entity-detail that gene-detail exhibits.

improvements.recommendation: Ensure the chip CSS pseudo-tooltip (data-tooltip ::before/::after) has a non-hover/focus equivalent or keep the aria-label as the sole accessible name, and verify chip background/text pairs (publication cyan #cffafe, genereview #dbeafe, variation #dcfce7) meet 4.5:1 with the #0f172a text.
improvements.expected_impact: Keeps the entity evidence chips accessible and contrast-compliant when the page renders.
